_meta.source: CEV Informe Final 'Hay Futuro Si Hay Verdad' (2022) — coined/load-bearing concepts across all 11 tomos

concepts.gloss: The 'great peace' — the report's animating ideal: not merely ending the State-FARC-EP confrontation but a national, deliberative peace that opens democracy, removes weapons from politics and confronts structural causes.
Non-repetition — the third name of the Commission and the telos of every recommendation: making the catastrophe structurally unrepeatable.

'War mode' — the cultural/cognitive condition in which Colombians, even unarmed, cannot conceive of the other thinking differently, converting opponents into enemies. Reconciliation requires desinstalar las narrativas de odio.
'Internal enemy' — the symmetrical Cold War doctrine (on right and left) that made the differing other an enemy with whom one neither negotiates nor tells the truth.
The mothers' name for civilians murdered by the Army and falsely presented as combat kills (≥6,402, 2002-2008). The demand '¿Quién dio la orden?' (Who gave the order?) seeks command responsibility.
'Even war has limits' — IHL/ius in bello binds all parties; there is no derecho a la guerra; war crimes and crimes against humanity have no justification.
'The greater the power, the greater the responsibility' — the graduated-responsibility doctrine: as guarantor of rights, the State bears the heaviest duty of all actors.

'Civilian third parties' — economic/political actors who sponsored, tolerated, financed or determined violence (paid paramilitaries to displace/dispossess); a fourth axis of responsibility beyond the armed actors.
The 'interwoven web' framing: the war as a complex web of political, economic, institutional, military and narco interests — not a clash of armed apparatuses.

'More territory than State' — the selective/absent State: present as coercion, absent as guarantor of rights, over vast peripheries that private and illegal capital colonize. See also dualidad territorial, el revés de la nación.
'Counter-agrarian reform' — the violent reversal of agrarian reform via massive dispossession, leader assassination and narco land accumulation.
'Re-primarization' of the export economy (oil, minerals, coca replacing coffee), temporally and spatially coincident with armed-group and illicit-crop growth.
'Urbanization of the war' — the city as battlefield, not refuge: intra-urban displacement, fronteras invisibles (invisible borders partitioning comunas), limpieza social.
The erasure of the peasantry as a sujeto político de derechos (political subject of rights) — itself a finding. The campesinado was the principal victim (45.5% of victimizing acts).

'Continuum of violences' — the conflict as the latest layer of violences rooted in trato colonial and racismo estructural (for ethnic peoples); and the historical/private-public/peace-war interconnection (for women).
concepts.volume: Resistir no es aguantar / Mi cuerpo es la verdad

concepts.gloss: 'Structural racism' — embedded in institutions, law, education and the founding narrative of the nation-state; armed actors assimilated and replicated it, causing disproportionate harm to ethnic peoples.

'Body-territory' — harm to a body is harm to the land and vice versa; territory itself is a legal victim (territorio como víctima, Decreto 4633/2011). For women, body and land are dominated by the same patriarchal power.
concepts.volume: Resistir no es aguantar / Mi cuerpo es la verdad

concepts.gloss: Ethnocide — cumulative violences risking the destruction of a people as a sujeto colectivo de derechos; peoples (Awá, Nukak) at risk of physical and cultural extinction (Auto 004/2009).
'Pervivencia' — continued existence/survival of a people; the ethnic-political goal that resistance defends.

Harm defined morally (Thiebaut) as a deliberately produced disequilibrium, not merely a rights-violation — described through the destruction of forms of life. Daños feed back and compound; many are irreparable.
concepts.volume: Sufrir la guerra y rehacer la vida

concepts.gloss: 'Biocultural trauma' — an integrated injury to people, the social fabric (tejido social) AND the land, caused jointly by armed actors and 'development actors'.
concepts.volume: Sufrir la guerra y rehacer la vida

concepts.gloss: 'Political genocide' (companion term: politicidio) — for the extermination of the Unión Patriótica (~5,000+ killed 1984-2005).
concepts.volume: Sufrir la guerra y rehacer la vida / Hallazgos

concepts.gloss: Coping (protect/survive/manage emotion) vs. resistance (confront/transform/build). The organizing axis of recovery; 'resistir no es aguantar' (to resist is not to endure).
concepts.volume: Sufrir la guerra y rehacer la vida / Resistir no es aguantar

concepts.gloss: Coming-together / collective solidarity — the women's mode of rebuilding the tejido social and doing peace ('no queremos ser pactadas, sino pactantes').
'The body, the first territory of peace' — the affirmative LGBTIQ+ resistance frame; the trans body as territorio de paz.
'Warrior masculinities' — the war-rewarded 'being a real man' common to paramilitaries, guerrillas and security forces; the report proposes demilitarizing masculinity.
'Reproductive violence' — violation of reproductive autonomy: forced contraception/sterilization/abortion (central inside guerrilla ranks), forced pregnancy/maternity.
'Prejudice-based violence' (not 'homophobia') — persecution for non-normative sexual orientation/gender identity, to impose a heteronormative/cisnormative order and win citizen legitimacy.
'Social cleansing' — extermination of those deemed socially 'undesirable' (LGBTIQ+ people, sex workers, drug users, street youth), often announced in panfletos.
concepts.volume: Mi cuerpo es la verdad / Colombia adentro

concepts.gloss: 'It is not a lesser evil' — the rebuke of framing child-harm as a minor/collateral matter; minors were a specific target, not incidental.

'Linkage' — umbrella for how minors were made to serve armed actors, split into reclutamiento (joining the ranks) and utilización (exploitation without incorporation). Recruitment was 'naturalized and intentional'.
'The Colombia outside Colombia' — the exiled population as a still-belonging part of the nation: 'no son víctimas que están fuera, sino que tuvieron que salir'.
'Insile / inner exile' — the experience of those who stayed behind, having lost something fundamental; the inverse banishment, affecting millions.
'Banishment / un-earthing' (life pulled from its course) and 'out-of-time / mistiming' (exile as a prolonged limbo that seems never to end).

Unarmed ethnic self-protection bodies carrying the bastón de mando — rescuing the kidnapped, blocking recruitment, searching for the disappeared. Rooted in cimarronaje (marronage). Emblem of unarmed territorial resistance.
'Prior consultation' (ILO 169 / Ley 21 of 1991) — the right of ethnic peoples to be consulted; the method by which the CEV's ethnic volume was itself built.

The paramilitary franchise template invented in Magdalena Medio: cattle ranchers + narcos + Army + politicians = durable, exportable paramilitarism. See also Convivir, parapolítica, Pacto de Ralito, connivencia.

'The event of truth' — truth as a public good and a public happening, evidenced equally by victims' courage and by the negacionismo, fake news and attacks against the Commission. The Commission does not own the truth ('le pertenece a la verdad').
'Strategic emptying' — depopulating territory to free land for agribusiness and extraction; dispossession as the war's product, not its byproduct.
concepts.volume: Colombia adentro / Hasta la guerra tiene límites

concepts.gloss: 'Human security' — the recommended security paradigm: security as a public good centered on people, life, communities and nature, replacing the war/enemy and drug-war paradigm.
